24 HOUR RAINFALL TOTAL 4.40 INCHES. HEAVIEST RAINS YESTERDAY EVENING PROMPTED EVACUATIONS OF PARTS OF ELLINGTON. EVACUEES WERE HOUSED AT LOCAL SCHOOL DURING THE FLOODING... BUT WERE ALLOWED TO RETURN HOME AFTER MIDNIGHT ONCE THE FLOODING SUBSIDED. TIME ESTIMATED.
LOTS OF FLOODING ON ROADS THAT NORMALLY DO NOT FLOOD.
SEVERAL VEHICLES ARE STALLING IN ROADWAYS DUE TO NEARLY TWO FEET OF WATER THROUGHOUT MANY ROADS AND INTERSECTIONS IN TOWN. CREEKS SPILLING OVER JUST WEST OF TOWN...CAUSING FLOODING ON IL ROUTE 161.
THE STREAM GAGE HEIGHT ON THE LOGAN CREEK IN ELLINGTON IS 13.15 FEET. THE SHERIFF REPORTS PORTIONS OF THE TOWN HAVE BEEN EVACUATED.
